text:
BOTTOM LINE — Oil bid, tech sold, metals ripped. Crude’s +2.20% jump from escalating US-Iran tensions lifted energy and materials but crushed tech — QQQ fell 0.51% — as the AI bull case cracks widened, confirmed by a defensive rotation into utilities XLU +2.25% and gold GLD +1.15%.

INDICES — VIX 16.6 below its 20-day MA of 16.8. Best: IWM -0.93%, DIA -0.01%, SPY -0.12%, QQQ -0.51%, SOXX +0.51%. Large > small by 0.42pp. Oil +2.20% led cross-asset.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.9x, QQQ vol_ratio 0.7x. QQQ’s low volume with price down signals low-conviction distribution — tech is bleeding quietly. XLU and XLE saw elevated volume with gains, showing real money rotating into energy and defensives.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLU Utilities +2.25%, XLB Materials +1.44%, XLE Energy +1.20%, XLP Staples +0.38%, XLI Industrials +0.11%, XLF Financials -0.11%, XLK Technology -0.28%, XLRE Real Estate -0.42%, XLV Healthcare -0.51%, XLY Discretionary -0.74%, XLC Comms -0.75%. Top Industries: GDX Gold Miners +3.36%, UTES Utilities +2.72%. Bottom: IGV Software -3.05%, KWEB China Internet -2.15%. Money flowing into metals and utilities, out of tech and discretionary — defensive rotation.

POSITIONING — SPY put-call ratio 1.40 elevated, demand for protection strong. QQQ put-call 1.53 even higher, with IV skew +13.3% signaling fear. SPY’s price near gamma flip at 756 adds downside amplification risk.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil’s +2.20% rise pressured airlines and discretionary while gold’s +1.15% rally alongside the dollar -0.11% signals a risk-off bid into haven metals, not typical inflation-driven gold.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 63.6 Greed. Top movers: NOW -6.47% (Software, distribution via vol spike 2.3x), GDX +3.36% (Gold Miners, accumulation), GLD +1.15% (Gold, accumulation). US-Iran war escalation drove oil and metals flows. Watch: NOW — vol spike and distribution below 200-day MA; IGV — -3.05% leading software collapse, watch for follow-through; XLU — +2.25% on elevated volume, clue for defensive rotation persistence.
